State of California

Memorandum

To      : Memo to File                                Date  : June 14, 1976

                                                       M-76-439

From  : Nan Hambleton
        FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Letter from W.E. Smith, City Clerk, City of Milpitas,
         dated May 14, 1976


         I telephoned Mr. Smith today.  Advised that
Gov. Code Sections 3600, et seq. apply now only to planning
commissioners and officers, and that Sections 87200-87207
apply to councilmen and city managers.  He had already
clarified with city attorney requirement of their ordinance
and also his questions re campaign disclosure re their
7/27/76 recall election.


E P P C
JUN 7 9:50AM'76

May 14, 1976

City Clerk


Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn.: Ms. Nan Hambleton
1100 K Street - P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Dear Ms. Hambleton:

Following is a request for an informal interpretation of
filing requirements pertaining to the Conflict of Interests
legislation.

Enclosed are copies of our ordinance on the subject; one
was passed by the Milpitas City Council and the other by the
Milpitas Sanitary District Board of Directors (an elected body).

Regarding "officials" as defined in Section 1-30-200 of
Ordinance No. 177, except for Councilmen, Commissioners, City
Manager, Planner and Assistant Planner, need these file annual
statements of Economic Interests (C.C. 87200-87207) or (P.C.
3600-3760) - or any statements at all?

With reference to Milpitas Sanitary District Ordinance No.
5, is the requirement for Disclosure Statements, annual (P.C.
3600-3760) applicable to Sanitary District Board members? (The
Milpitas Sanitary District is a separate legal entity. The
board members earn less than $100 per month).

Also enclosed is a schedule of reporting forms for office
holders which I drew up for my own guidance. I would appreciate
your looking it over for accuracy.

Sincerely yours,


W. E. Smith
encl.                                    City Clerk


6-3-76 Reply and yet received.
[illegible]


City of Milpitas
455 E. Calaveras Blvd.
Milpitas, California 95035
(408) 262-2310

June 2, 1976

Mr. Haskell M. Goodman
210 N. 4th Street
San Jose, CA 95112

Dear Jack:

There is an apparent discrepancy in deadline dates for proponents of
recall measures in instructions of the Fair Political Practices Commission
as applied to our Special Recall Election scheduled for July 27, 1976.

Under III.B. (page 4) of the instructions (copy enclosed), committees
must file the first of two pre-election campaign statements by either
the 40th or the 35th day prior to the election. This would be June 17,
1976 or June 22, 1976 respectively. Under III.E. (page 5), it states
that the deadline for filing by proponents is 65 days after the date of
qualification of the petition. Since the petition was declared qualified
on April 22, 1976, this would be June 26, 1976.

Does this mean that three pre-election campaign statements are due from
the proponents or only that one is required in a recall election?

Incidentally, in the schedule I drew up, I used the same dates for the
committee as for the "recallee", who is classified as a "candidate". This
is the 40th and 12th day prior to the election for the pre-election
statements and 65 days after the election for the post-election statement.

Please advise if a change in instructions to the proponents will be
necessary.

Sincerely,

W. E. Smith
CITY CLERK

WES/C22/4

Enc.

cc: Fair Political Practices Commission

224 E. Coleraine Blvd.
Millvale, Chittamo 30322
(904) 35-5101

June 8, 1984

Mrs. Russell M. Goodman
311 W.A. Shafter
San Jose, Ca 95110

Dear Mrs. Goodman:

I want to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation for the wonderful gift of the first Hallmark greeting card collection that you presented to me. While I can only vaguely recall my visit to you, the gift certainly brought back many pleasant memories.

Under U.D. (page 4) of one presentation amendment, as per his orders, I was enabled to practice on the same date. I then sent some five miles to the new homes and have been contemplating returning to work on the Bud's base at the same site. (A) Under the past several years I had suggested at the same time the work assignment I have since accomplished. Under article 15, I send you this note as in response for your kind cooperation to make at the business reasonable understanding possible.

Since I knew in which department was beneficial is in reasonable understanding it as in response to the proposition while my wife and more most

At the book level I shall have noted to give a working case as in response for the back extension.

Please allow it a guide to understand at the proposition while it will be

Respectively,

W.E. Smit
City Guest

a calendar year, she is a "Major Donor Committee" and must file periodic reports. She would report the $500 as contributions to the various committees, and the committees would report the money as contributions from Joan Q. Smith.

D. Proponents of Ballot Measures

The proponents of ballot measures have special

filing requirements whether or not they qualify as committees, and must file campaign statements. Proponents must file campaign statements after qualifying a measure for the ballot. If the measure fails to qualify, they still must file after the final deadline for circulating petitions.

II. WHAT FORMS MUST BE FILED?

A. Candidates and Elected Officeholders

Form 470—Candidates Campaign Statement,
Short Form: This form may be used only by those candidates and elected officeholders who have neither raised nor spent more than $200 or in whose behalf not more than $200 has been raised or spent since the date of the most recent post-election statement or since January 7, 1975 if the candidate or elected officeholder has not previously filed a post-election statement.

Form 430—Candidates Campaign Statement: This form must be used by all candidates and elected officeholders not eligible to use the short Form 470.

B. Committees

Form 410—Statement of Organization: This form must be filed by all committees which receive contributions of $500 or more during a calendar year ("Recipient Committees"). "Expenditure Committees" and "Major Donor Committees" do not file Statements of Organization.

Form 420—Committee Campaign Statement:
This form may be used by any committee. However, committees which receive a cumulative contribution of $50 or more from a single source must file this form.

Form 440—Major Donor Campaign Statement:
This form may only be used by "Major Donor Committees."

Form 450—Association Campaign Statement:
This form may be used by "Recipient Committees" if no cumulative contribution of $50 or more has been received from any single source since the committee filed its last post-election statement. If single source contributions of $50 or more have been received, Form 420 must be used.

Form 460—Committee Short Form: This form may only be used by "Expenditure Committees."

C. Proponents of Ballot Measures

Forms 420 or 450: These forms may be used by proponents of ballot measures.

III. WHEN MUST STATEMENTS BE FILED?

Statements must be received by the appropriate filing officer by the filing deadline. It is not sufficient to send the statements by ordinary mail post-marked as of the filing deadline. However, statements mailed first-class mail (not certified mail) will be considered received on the date of the postmark.

A. Statement of Organization—FORM 410

Any person or entity receiving $500 or more in contributions in a calendar year is a "Recipient Committee" and must file a Statement of Organization within ten days of such receipt.

B. Pre-election Campaign Statements and Reports of Late Contributions

Candidates and committees supporting or opposing candidates must file two pre-election campaign statements, the first by the 40th day and the second by the 12th day prior to the election. (See Section D below for special and run-off election filing schedules.)

Committees supporting or opposing ballot measures must file two pre-election campaign statements,

the first by the 35th day and the second by the 7th day prior to the election.

Committees which support or oppose both candidates and ballot measures may choose the filing schedule for either candidates or ballot measures, but they must follow one schedule consistently.

The closing date for pre-election campaign statements is three days before the filing deadline. Pre-election statements may not be filed before the closing date.

Late Contributions: Any contribution of $1,000 or more received prior to the election, but after the "closing date" of the last pre-election statement, must be reported within 48 hours to the appropriate filing officer. It must be reported by any written means of communication, such as telegram or letter. The late contribution report does not have to contain an original signature but must contain the name, street address, occupation and employer, or principal place of business, if self-employed, of the contributor. The same information must be reported again on the next campaign statement filed.


C. Post-election Campaign Statements

Candidates and committees supporting or opposing candidates must file campaign statements not later than 65 days after the election.

Committees supporting or opposing ballot measures must file campaign statements not later than 70 days after the election.

The closing date for post-election campaign statements is seven days prior to the filing deadline. However, if prior to the closing date, all liabilities have been paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are expected, the campaign statement may be filed at any time after the election and before the deadline. The early filing applies only to post-election statements.

Once a candidate or committee files a pre-election statement, each must continue to file until a post-election statement is filed, even if there are no further transactions.

D. Special Elections and Run-offs

A different schedule for the filing of campaign statements must be followed in the case of a special election or any other election for an office for which there is, or may be, a run-off within sixty days of the first election.

The pre-election statements are due:
33 days prior to the first election;
7 days prior to the first election;
7 days prior to the run-off.

The post-election statement is due:
65 days after the run-off.

The closing date for the three pre-election statements is three days before the filing deadline. The closing date for the post run-off statement is seven days before the due date. However, if prior to the closing date, all liabilities have been paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are expected, the campaign statement may be filed at any time after the election and before the deadline. The early filing applies only to post-election statements.

A candidate who is unsuccessful in the special primary election and is not a candidate in the run-off election and committees supporting such a candidate, need not file the campaign statement between the primary and the run-off. But such candidate and committees must file a post-election statement no later than 65 days after the final election. The candidate and committees may not file until after the run-off election.

E. Ballot Measure Proponents

A proponent of an initiative, referendum or recall measure must file a campaign statement following the circulation of petitions. The statement must be filed, whether or not the proponent is successful. If the initiative, referendum or recall is successful in qualifying, the deadline for filing is 65 days after the date of qualification. If not successful, the deadline is

65 days after the final date for circulating petitions. In either case, the closing date is seven days prior to the deadline for filing the campaign statement.

F. "Off-Year" Statements

Elected Officeholders: An elected officeholder must file semiannual campaign statements during off-election years. The statements cover six-month periods, and must be filed whether or not the officeholder received contributions or made expenditures. However, if the officeholder was required to file a campaign statement in connection with other election or elections held during a six-month period, the "off-year" statement need not be filed at the end of that period.

Elected officeholders whose salaries are less than $600 in a six-month period, and judges need not file "off-year" campaign statements unless they receive contributions or make expenditures during the period.

Candidates: All candidates who are not officeholders are required to file "off-year" campaign statements if they have received contributions, made expenditures or given their consent for any other person to receive contributions or make expenditures with the aim of bringing about their nomination or election to any elective office. However, if they were required to file campaign statements in connection with any election or elections held during the six-month period, they need not file an "off-year" statement for that period.

Committees: All committees, "Recipient," "Expenditure" and "Major Donor," supporting or opposing state and local candidates or opposing ballot measures must file "off-year" campaign statements if they have received contributions or if they have made expenditures in support of or in opposition to candidates or measures. However, if they were required to file campaign statements in connection with any election or elections held during the six-month period, they need not file an "off-year" statement for that period.

The schedule for "off-year" statements is:

Period Covered                                    Due By
January 1 through June 30 .........................July 31
July 1 through December 31 .................January 31

Reminder: If the elected officeholder, candidate or committee files a statement within one of these six-month periods in connection with any election during the period, no "off-year" statement is due for that particular six-month period. The next "off-year" statement would be due after the end of the succeeding six-month period and would cover the time from the closing date of the last post-election filing to the end of the most recent six-month period.

G. Unopposed Incumbent Candidates for Judicial Office

Incumbent candidates for judicial offices whose names do not appear on the ballot because they are

5


5/10/76


CITY OF MILPITAS
ADMINISTRATIVE CALENDAR
SPECIAL RECALL ELECTION - JULY 27, 1976

------

APRIL 21    Certification of signatures on recall petition received from Registrar
            of Voters.

            Prepare Clerk's certificate and attach to petition.

APRIL 22    Forward copy of certification to proponents of the recall.

            Agendize for Council meeting of May 4.  Election to be ordered
            not less than 74 nor more than 89 days.  Recommend July 27.

            Prepare resolution calling special election.

APRIL 23    Order Spanish translation for Notice of Election.

MAY   6     Order Notice of Election to be published May 12.  (May 19 last day).
            Request services of Registrar from Board of Supervisors.

MAY   10    Order Spanish translations for ballot, statement and answer.


            Call and/or send inquiries to prospective election officers;
            apply for use of schools.  Send preliminary list of consolidated
            precincts to Registrar.
            Obtain precinct count.

MAY   14    Give Purchasing information to start bidding procedure for ballots
            (official paper must be obtained from Secretary of State).

            Order precinct supplies.

MAY   27    Order ballot measures published June 2 and 9 (July 7 and 14 last days).

JUNE  7     Order sample ballots, statement and answer (for delivery June 17).
            Order official and absent voter ballots (for delivery June 28).

            Agendize appointment of precinct boards, polling places, salaries
            and resolution for Clerk to canvass vote for June 15 Council meeting.

            Order Spanish translation for Notice of Appointment Election
            Officers and Designation of Polling Places (See San Jose Post-
            Record of Feb. 13, 1976 for form).

JUNE 10     Mail campaign statement forms to candidate and committees.


Special Recall Election - July 27, 1976 (cont.)


JUNE 17    Deliver sample ballots, statement and answer to Registrar.
           Order list of election officers, polling places and hours published
           June 23 (July 7 last day).

           Mail election officer appointments, Digests and Inspector's
           Declaration forms.  Send list of consolidated precincts and polling
           places to Registrar.

           First campaign statements due (thru June 14).

JUNE 27    Registration closes.

           Memo to P.W. Dept. (Parks) to arrange for pickup and delivery of
           election supplies to polling places.

           Pick up Indices from Registrar.

JUNE 28   Pick up official and absent voter ballots (if not already delivered).
thru      Mail out absent voter ballots (including those in Precinct 4403 -
JULY 20   not consolidated).
          Check that all Inspector's Declarations are in.

JULY 8     Mail campaign statement forms to candidate and committees.

JULY 15    Second campaign statements due (thru July 12).

JULY 16    Last day to mail sample ballots and separate printed copies of
           statement of proponents and answer of the officer sought to be
           recalled.  (By Registrar).

           Make up election officers' payroll.

JULY 19    Make up receipts for precinct supplies.
           Check to see that Registrar has received precinct supplies.

JULY 22    Prepare agenda item for August 3 Council meeting - election results.

JULY 23    Pickup and deliver booths, ballot boxes and precinct books to polling
           places and Inspectors' homes.

JULY 27    ELECTION DAY.

JULY 28    Return precinct books and equipment.
           Canvass vote.

JULY 30    Prepare certificate of results.
           (If another election is necessary to fill vacancy in the event the
           recall prevails, it could be consolidated with the November 2
           statewide election).

SEPT 23    Mail campaign statement forms to candidate and committees.

SEPT 30    Third campaign statements due (thru Sept. 23).


[illegible]
